text: A person's monthly budget of Rs. 1,20,000 is split as Education Rs. 32,000, Food Rs. 43,000, Entertainment Rs. 12,000, Travelling Rs. 17,000 and Other Rs. 16,000. The percentage each family member spends of each item is -- P: Edu 10, Food 30, Ent 10, Travel 40, Other 20. W: 15, 25, 30, 10, 25. Rahul: 40, 20, 20, 25, 20. Rohit: 25, 15, 25, 10, 10. Preeti: 10, 10, 15, 15, 25. What are the average expenses of P (across the five items)?

A monthly budget of Rs. 1,20,000 is split as Education 32,000, Food 43,000, Entertainment 12,000, Travelling 17,000, Other 16,000. Each member's share (%) of each item -- P: 10/30/10/40/20; W: 15/25/30/10/25; Rahul: 40/20/20/25/20; Rohit: 25/15/25/10/10; Preeti: 10/10/15/15/25 (Edu/Food/Ent/Travel/Other). What is the approximate percentage increase in the amount Rahul spends on Entertainment compared with Preeti?
explanation: Rahul's Entertainment = 20% of 12000 = 2400; Preeti's = 15% of 12000 = 1800. Increase = 600/1800 = 33.3%.

text: A monthly budget of Rs. 1,20,000 is split as Education 32,000, Food 43,000, Entertainment 12,000, Travelling 17,000, Other 16,000. Member shares (%) -- P: 10/30/10/40/20; W: 15/25/30/10/25; Rahul: 40/20/20/25/20; Rohit: 25/15/25/10/10; Preeti: 10/10/15/15/25. The average expenses of Rohit are approximately what percent of the average expenses of W?

A monthly budget of Rs. 1,20,000 is split as Education 32,000, Food 43,000, Entertainment 12,000, Travelling 17,000, Other 16,000. Member shares (%) -- P: 10/30/10/40/20; W: 15/25/30/10/25 (Edu/Food/Ent/Travel/Other). Find the difference (as a percentage of the total budget) between the average Education expense and the average Entertainment expense of the couple (P and W).
explanation: Couple's Education = 25% of 32000 = 8000 (avg 4000); Entertainment = 40% of 12000 = 4800 (avg 2400). Difference 1600 is 1600/120000 = 1.33%.

text: A monthly budget of Rs. 1,20,000 is split as Education 32,000, Food 43,000, Entertainment 12,000, Travelling 17,000, Other 16,000. Member shares (%) -- Rahul: Edu 40, Food 20, Ent 20, Travel 25, Other 20; Preeti: Edu 10, Food 10, Ent 15, Travel 15, Other 25. The total amount spent by Rahul on Travelling and Food is approximately what percent of the total spent by Preeti on Education and Food?

Read the data and answer. Sales (Rs. Lakhs): Company A -- 2021:120, 2022:140, 2023:180, 2024:200. Company B -- 150, 160, 170, 190. Company C -- 100, 120, 150, 180. Company D -- 130, 150, 160, 170. In 2022, what percentage of the total sales was contributed by Company B?

Read the data and answer. Sales (Rs. Lakhs): Company A -- 120,140,180,200; Company B -- 150,160,170,190; Company C -- 100,120,150,180; Company D -- 130,150,160,170 (for 2021-2024). Which company had the highest percentage growth in sales from 2021 to 2024?
explanation: Growth: A 66.7%, B 26.7%, C 80%, D 30.8%. Company C grew the most (100 to 180).

text: Read the data and answer. Sales (Rs. Lakhs): Company A -- 120,140,180,200; Company B -- 150,160,170,190; Company C -- 100,120,150,180; Company D -- 130,150,160,170 (for 2021-2024). In which year did Company D contribute the maximum share to the total sales?
Read the data and answer. Sales (Rs. Lakhs): Company A -- 120,140,180,200; Company C -- 100,120,150,180 (for 2021-2024). What is the ratio of the total 4-year sales of Company A to that of Company C?
Read the data and answer. In 2024 Company B's sales were Rs. 190 Lakhs. If the profit margin of Company B was 20% in 2024, what was its profit that year (Rs. Lakhs)?
A survey of 1,200 students on preferred mode of transport gives: Walking 10%, Car 25%, Bicycle 20%, Bus 30%, Train 15%. How many students prefer traveling by car?
A survey of 1,200 students gives: Walking 10%, Car 25%, Bicycle 20%, Bus 30%, Train 15%. If 10% of bus users switch to train, what will be the new percentage of train users?
explanation: Bus = 360; 10% (36) switch to train. Train becomes 180+36 = 216, i.e., 216/1200 = 18%.

text: A survey of 1,200 students gives: Walking 10%, Car 25%, Bicycle 20%, Bus 30%, Train 15%. What is the ratio of students using bus to those walking?
A survey of 1,200 students gives: Walking 10%, Car 25%, Bicycle 20%, Bus 30%, Train 15%. If the number of bicycle users increases by 25%, what will be their new count?
